name: Bill Frisell
description: '''William Richard Frisell''' ({{IPAc-en|f|r|ɪ|ˈ|z|ɛ|l}} {{respell|fri|ZEL}}; born March 18, 1951) is an American [[jazz]] guitarist. He first came to prominence at [[ECM Records]] in the 1980s, as both a [[Session musician|session player]] and a leader. He went on to work in a variety of contexts, notably as a participant in the Downtown Scene in New York City, where he formed a long working relationship with composer and saxophonist [[John Zorn]]. He was also a longtime member of veteran drummer [[Paul Motian]]'s groups from the early 1980s until Motian's death in 2011. Since the late 1990s, Frisell's output as a bandleader has also integrated prominent elements of [[folk music|folk]], [[country music|country]], [[Rock and roll|rock 'n' roll]], and [[Americana music|Americana]]. He holds six [[Grammy Awards|Grammy]] nominations and one win.
